pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif. Standard Fiji/ImageJ sample image containing 64 labeled objects.
setup_task.sh script lines 8-9: DATA_DIR=/home/ga/ImageJ_Data, RESULTS_DIR=$DATA_DIR/results. File cleared on line 17.

Task description requires 'Mark at least 5 Round objects... Mark at least 5 Elongated objects'. Verifier.py line 136 checks: 'valid_counts = [c for c in counts if c >= 5]' and requires len(valid_counts) >= 2.

verifier.py line 103-105 checks 'if round_mean > 0.85' for good circularity. Line 13 in docstring states 'Round class should have high circularity (>0.85 approx)'. Blobs image analysis shows 44 objects with circularity > 0.85 (mean 1.014).

verifier.py line 112-114 checks 'if elongated_mean < 0.80' for good circularity. Blobs image analysis shows 15 objects with circularity <= 0.80 (mean 0.666), providing clear morphological distinction from round objects.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the 'Blobs (25K)' image, a published ImageJ sample containing 64 objects with clearly distinct morphologies: 44 round objects (circularity > 0.85) and 15 elongated objects (circularity <= 0.80). Output is saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/manual_counts.csv. Minimum of 5 points per class is required. The verifier checks circularity thresholds of 0.85 for round and 0.80 for elongated objects.
